title: Contestant Gates
desc: The fighter gates are sticking again, which is bad for scheduling and worse for drama.

steps.text:
Every bout at the Colosseum begins the same way: two gates, two fighters, one moment when the bars lift and the crowd decides whose entrance it prefers. That moment loses a great deal of force if one gate sticks halfway up or groans like a dying furnace on the pull. This morning the east-side mechanisms are dragging badly enough that the gate handler has stopped pretending it might work itself out.

She kicks the lower track with professional contempt. "Track grit or hinge teeth," she says. "Clear the sand packed into the base if you want the filthy job, or service the upper hinges and lift teeth if you'd rather climb above the bars. Either way, I need a clean rise before noon."

options.label: Clear the lower track and packed grit at the base
Climb up and service the hinge teeth above the gate

steps.text:
The lower track is a perfect collector of everything the arena produces in excess: sand, grit, flakes of rust, and the occasional chip of bone nobody was paid enough to notice last night. You pry the worst of it free, brush the remaining dust from the channel, and work the bars up and down in short pulls until the drag changes from grinding resistance to ordinary weight.

When the handler tests the full lift, the gate rises straight enough to satisfy even a crowd that enjoys finding faults. No hitch at the midpoint, no sideways jerk in the guides, no embarrassing pause for the fighter behind it. She lets the bars fall once more and listens to the clean iron slam at the base. "Better," she says. "Now the entrances can go back to looking inevitable."

options.label: Sweep the loosened grit aside and clear the lane

steps.text:
Above the gate, the lift assembly is all teeth, pins, and iron links blackened by heat and old smoke. Two teeth on the east-side rack have worn enough at the corners to catch against the lift pawl unless the whole line is pulled with more force than anyone likes using over a fighter's head. You clean the teeth, file the worst burrs back to shape, and oil the hinge points while the handler works the lever below in slow controlled motions.

By the time you finish, the mechanism no longer sounds offended to be operating. It rises and settles with the heavy certainty the Colosseum likes to project about everything. The handler watches one full practice lift, then another, and finally allows herself a short, satisfied grin. "Good. A gate should either stay shut or open dramatically. Nothing in between."

options.label: Cap the oil flask and leave the mechanism behaving properly
